column1: # /Worksheet Titles — freeze rows above and/or columns left of the
# cell pointer.  Both / Horizontal / Vertical / Clear branches.  The
# renderer already consults `frozen[sheet]`; this exercises the menu
# Seed two row labels (A1, A2) and one column label (B1) so we can
# assert what stays pinned after we scroll.
# Move to A3 (row index 2) and freeze rows above with /WTH.
# Drive the pointer ~30 rows down to force vertical scroll.  The
# frozen prefix (rows 0 and 1) must still render at the top.
# Alt-F4 undoes the freeze; with no titles the row 1+2 labels scroll
# off the top once the pointer is past them.
# Back to A1 and exercise /WTC on a sheet without titles — the
# command is a quiet no-op (no journal entry, no error).
# /WTV at C1 freezes columns A and B left of the pointer.  Scroll
# right and assert column A keeps painting Title1.
